rulings.Case Title: DAVID ROBERTS VS. ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Of Keenan Properties, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 4, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Keenan Properties Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is continued on the court's own motion to February 11, 2015. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND TOWNSEND et al VS. ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication As To Plaintiffs Request For Punitive Damages
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 4, 2015 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Gardner Denver, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JOPSEPH ANATHONY GARZA et al VS. JOHN CRANE, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Of Defendant John Crane Inc.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 4, 2015 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant John Crane Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. The declarations of Robert Garritano and John Fening create a triable issue whether decedent Joseph Garza was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to decedent's deposition testimony are sustained. Defendant's objections to the Garritano and Fening declarations are overruled. While defendant's Notice of Motion identified a Robert Whalen, defendant's moving papers as a whole, including its memorandum and separate statement, made clear that it was moving for summary judgment on the grounds that there is no evidence that decedent Joseph Garza was exposed to an asbestos-containing John Crane product. (See, e.g., Luri v. Greenwald (2003) 107 Cal.App.4th 1119. 1125.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
